# GitHub Repo Topics (for discoverability)

GitHub repository **topics** are surfaced in search, in the repo card, and in
GitHub's "Explore" page. They are configured in the repo's **Settings → About →
Topics** field (not in any tracked file). This file documents the topics this
repo intentionally claims.

## Suggested topics for `armpro24-blip/aieng-ui`

```
cad, cae, fea, engineering, fastapi, react, three-js, workbench, ai, llm,
agent, mcp, calculix, freecad, runtime, approval-gate, audit, evidence,
design-automation, web-app
```

20 topics — the GitHub maximum.

## Suggested GitHub repository description

```
Local web workbench and FastAPI runtime for the .aieng engineering platform — approval-gated CAE adapter, artifact write-back, evidence review API, and MCP-compatible tool surface for LLM agents driving CAD/CAE workflows.
```

## Suggested website field

```
https://github.com/armpro24-blip/aieng-ui#readme
```

## Why these topics

- `fastapi`, `react`, `three-js`, `web-app` — the technology stack, key for
  discovery by web-dev folks who might integrate with us.
- `cad`, `cae`, `fea`, `engineering`, `calculix`, `freecad` — the domain.
- `ai`, `llm`, `agent`, `mcp` — the audience.
- `workbench`, `runtime`, `approval-gate`, `audit`, `evidence` — the
  distinctive product features (approval-gated execution, auditable trails).
- `design-automation` — the long-term positioning.

## Updating these topics

When the project pivots, update this file *and* the GitHub repo settings
together.
